_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 10 - Prada lexicon: models (with debut year + era) + materials/signature-motifs (TWO DNAs: Saffiano leather + nylon/Re-Nylon, plus the inverted-triangle 'dal 1913' logo plaque) + named colors/signatures

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). TWO load-bearing facts: (1) the Re-Edition bags are named by ARCHIVE YEAR (Re-Edition 1995/2000/2005 = the year the original debuted, the GEO decoder); (2) Miuccia Prada is the long-time designer, Raf Simons joined as co-creative-director 2020, so post-2020 bags (Cleo SS2021, Symbole 2022, Arque 2023, Moon revival) are the Miuccia+Raf era.
types.material: signature motif / leather / fabric / logo. TWO house DNAs: SAFFIANO (cross-hatch treated leather, invented+patented by Mario Prada 1913, the Galleria flagship) AND NYLON (Tessuto, the Vela backpack 1984, 'an emblem of the brand's DNA'; Re-Nylon = the recycled Econyl version, launched 2019). Plus the inverted-triangle enamel logo plaque (stamped 'dal 1913', the triangle Mario Prada first used on vintage trunks) + Vitello calf leathers + spazzolato + antique nappa + raffia.
types.color: named color / signature. KEY HONEST FINDING: Prada does NOT publish a per-season color-name lexicon (the Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine/Fendi pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Colors are plain descriptors, often flat Italian shade names (Rosa=pink, Cammeo=cameo nude, Talco=talc/off-white); naming weight sits on the MODEL + the MATERIAL (Saffiano vs nylon) + the TRIANGLE plaque, not the color. official_or_descriptive marks each.

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. TWO DNAs: Saffiano leather (Mario Prada patent 1913, the Galleria) + nylon (Tessuto/Vela 1984, Re-Nylon/Econyl 2019). The Re-Edition bags are named by archive year (1995/2000/2005) - the GEO decoder. Miuccia Prada is the long-time designer; Raf Simons co-CD from 2020. COLORS: Prada does NOT name its per-season colors (same finding as Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine/Fendi) - logged honestly, NOT invented; descriptors are often flat Italian shade names (Rosa/Cammeo/Talco). HELD RIGOR: a standalone 'Triangle bag' model + the 'Double Bag' did NOT surface with a clean source - logged low/lead, NOT fabricated (the triangle LOGO plaque is fully sourced as a material). The 'Pocone'/'Pocono' nylon trade name did NOT surface - logged as an unverified beat lead, NOT stated as fact (sourced nylon terms are Tessuto + Re-Nylon/Econyl). 'BR4651-era classics' is a style-code prefix, not a model name - NOT logged as a model. prada.com retail site NOT scraped wholesale (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci/BV/YSL/Celine/Fendi); pradagroup.com corporate sustainability page scraped clean (official Re-Nylon source). Sources: Vogue 'Prada Handbags 101' (the WORKHORSE, debut years), Fashionphile Academy '10 Styles' (Bowler/Cahier/Dynamique/Symbole years + 2000-vs-2005 strap distinction), Luxe Digital Jan-2026 buyer's guide (per-model release years + Promenade=Saffiano Lux Tote + Sidonie/Arque/Moon), Prada Group sustainability (Re-Nylon official), Rebag (Saffiano 1913 patent).
conflicts: 'Saffiano Lux Tote' name: the BRIEF associates it with the Galleria; Luxe Digital ties 'Saffiano Lux Tote' to the PROMENADE - logged the Promenade=Saffiano Lux Tote per the source, noted the brief framing in-row
Re-Edition 2005 reissue year: Vogue says 'late 2019' (reprised w/ 2000); Luxe Digital says '~2020' - logged late-2019/2020
Moon debut: Vogue says 'first debuted spring 2002 collection'; revived 2022 (Luxe Digital) - both captured, not a conflict

coverage.models: 16 model rows (Galleria, Re-Edition 1995/2000/2005, Cleo, Symbole, Arque, Moon, Cahier, Promenade/Saffiano Lux Tote, Sidonie, Re-Nylon Backpack/Vela, Bowler, Dynamique Hobo, Logo Tote, Nappa Antique Tote) + 2 honest leads (Triangle bag, Double Bag) logged low not invented; debut/archive years sourced for all 16
coverage.materials: 8 motifs/materials (Saffiano leather + nylon/Tessuto + Re-Nylon/Econyl as the TWO DNAs; the inverted-triangle 'dal 1913' logo plaque; Vitello calf + spazzolato + antique nappa + raffia)
coverage.colors: 6 plain-descriptor rows (Rosa/Cammeo/Talco Italian shade names + cherry-red/inky-black/millennial-pink editorial descriptors) + an explicit 'no per-season color lexicon exists' finding. NO invented seasonal color names.

name: Galleria
note: THE Prada leather flagship: structured rectangular Saffiano tote, two top handles + two zip closures + center compartment, 83 hand-finished pieces, medicine-bag inspired; named after the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II (Prada's first 1913 store). Triple-sourced: Vogue (2007, 83 pieces) + Fashionphile (2007, name from 1913 Galleria store) + Luxe Digital (2007).

name: Re-Edition 1995
note: Archive-year naming: a Fall 1995 archive top-handle bag, Galleria-like in brushed leather, restrained logo; reissued 2022; a Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy quiet-luxury favorite. Double-sourced: Vogue (debuted FW1995) + Luxe Digital (reissued 2022 from 1995 archive, CBK). Palette: cherry red / slick black / powdery blue + pink (descriptors).

name: Re-Edition 2000
note: Archive-year naming: an early-2000s nylon shoulder bag, reprised late 2019 in recycled Re-Nylon (Econyl). The 2000 has a FABRIC strap (the 2005 has a Saffiano-leather strap) - the key 2000-vs-2005 distinction. Double-sourced: Vogue (2000+2005 reprised late 2019) + Fashionphile (strap distinction).

name: Re-Edition 2005
note: Archive-year naming: THE breakout Y2K-revival nylon shoulder bag; triangle logo, woven strap + removable chain strap + removable Re-Nylon zip pouch; the 2005 has a SAFFIANO-leather strap. Reprised late 2019/2020 in Econyl Re-Nylon. Triple-sourced: Vogue (reprised late 2019, chain strap + pouch) + Luxe Digital (reissued ~2020, the breakout Y2K bag) + Fashionphile (Saffiano leather strap). Reissue-year conflict flagged: Vogue late-2019 vs Luxe Digital ~2020.

name: Cleo
designer: Miuccia Prada (a year shy of Raf Simons' 2020 co-CD appointment per Vogue)
note: Sleek curved shoulder bag, seamless 1990s lines, no hardware noise; brushed calfskin or lightweight spazzolato calf; flap or interior-snap silhouette; mini + regular. Debuted in the Multiple Views Spring/Summer 2021 collection. Triple-sourced: Vogue (SS2021 Multiple Views, spazzolato) + Fashionphile ('90s lines) + Luxe Digital (2021). Palette: inky black / milky white / powdery pistachio (descriptors).

name: Symbole
note: Boxy structured tote with a clochette + the triptych 'Symbole' jacquard of the triangle motif Mario Prada first used on the house's vintage trunks; lined in Re-Nylon; Large/Medium/Small/Mini + a camera-bag iteration. Debuted summer 2022. Double-sourced: Luxe Digital (summer 2022, triptych, clochette) + Fashionphile (2022, triangle from Mario Prada trunks, Re-Nylon lined).

name: Arque (Arque)
note: Sleek structured crescent/curved shoulder-or-crossbody bag drawn from an archival 2000s curve. Introduced 2023. Single-sourced (Luxe Digital, Jan 2026). Spelled Arque/Arque.

name: Moon
note: Soft crescent under-the-arm bag with a rectangular silver buckle + grommets 'borrowed from the sailing world'; Re-Nylon or puffed nappa leather. First debuted spring 2002 collection (year=2002 archive); revived 2022. Double-sourced: Vogue (first debuted spring 2002) + Luxe Digital (revived 2022 from early-2000s shape). Palette: millennial pink to acid green + browns/blacks (descriptors).

name: Cahier
note: Boxy book-/trunk-shaped bag ('Cahier' = French for 'notebook') with ornate antique-inspired metal corners + a front buckle; Saffiano or velvet. First released in the Fall/Winter 2016 collection. Double-sourced: Luxe Digital (FW2016, 'notebook' in French) + Fashionphile (2016, boxy trunk-like w/ front buckle).

name: Promenade (Saffiano Lux Tote)
note: Soft structured Saffiano tote, ALSO KNOWN AS the 'Saffiano Lux Tote'; similar to the Galleria but more feminine (curved corners, gentler silhouette); protective metal feet + detachable strap. Created 2007. Single-sourced (Luxe Digital). NOTE: the brief associated 'Saffiano Lux Tote' with the GALLERIA; this source ties the 'Saffiano Lux Tote' name to the PROMENADE - flagged for verification.

name: Sidonie
note: Vintage-inspired flap shoulder/crossbody with an oversized buckle nodding to old Prada travel trunks; detachable shoulder + crossbody straps; Saffiano. Introduced for Fall/Winter 2018. Double-sourced: Luxe Digital (FW2018) + The Luxury Closet (Sidonie in Saffiano w/ gold rings).

name: Re-Nylon Backpack (Vela)
note: The original Prada nylon backpack, launched as the 'Vela' in 1984 (the first application of Miuccia's nylon); rebranded the 'Re-Nylon Backpack' on its 2019 Econyl reintroduction; the '10 Things I Hate About You' Prada backpack. Triple-sourced: Vogue (Vela 1984, Re-Nylon since 2019) + Fashionphile (Tessuto Nylon Backpack 1984, the Vela) + Luxe Digital (Re-Nylon 2019). Year=1984 (Vela debut); Re-Nylon reintroduction 2019.

name: Bowler
note: Elongated rectangular dome-shaped 'bowling bag'; an instant early-2000s hit (nearly sold out on release), much-imitated by newer Prada shapes. Introduced 2000. Single-sourced (Fashionphile).

name: Dynamique Hobo
note: Soft lightweight no-fuss hobo with a magnetic top closure; runs in Vitello Daino leather. Introduced 2020. Single-sourced (Fashionphile).

name: Logo Tote (Raffia)
note: Woven raffia tote with an embroidered Prada logo; a summer/vacation tote in many colors. Introduced 2021. Single-sourced (Fashionphile).

name: Nappa Antique Tote
note: Roomy tote in a crinkly antique nappa leather wrinkled by hand (labor-intensive); small/medium 'portrait' + large 'landscape' orientations. Debuted Spring/Summer 2023. Single-sourced (Vogue, the workhorse).

name: Triangle bag
note: HONEST LEAD, NOT INVENTED: a small nylon pouch/shoulder bag fronted by the inverted-triangle plaque; 'Triangle' is used in the market for the triangle-logo nylon minis. The triangle LOGO plaque itself is fully sourced (see material rows). Whether 'Triangle bag' is an official model name vs a market descriptor is unresolved this run - logged low, no fabricated year. Queue prada.com / PurseForum.

name: Double Bag
note: HONEST LEAD, NOT INVENTED: a structured Saffiano tote with a double-compartment build; may be a market descriptor for the Galleria's twin-zip construction rather than a distinct model. Not cleanly sourced this run - logged low, no fabricated year. Queue prada.com / PurseForum.

name: Saffiano leather
note: THE Prada leather DNA: calf leather with a cross-hatch treated finish (scratch-resistant, shiny), invented + patented by Mario Prada in 1913; the material of the Galleria flagship. Triple-sourced: Vogue (patented by Mario Prada) + Luxe Digital (patented by Mario Prada) + Rebag (invented + patented by Mario Prada 1913) + prada.com (Galleria born 2007 in Saffiano).

name: Nylon (Tessuto)
note: THE other Prada DNA: utilitarian woven nylon Miuccia elevated to luxury ('she did to nylon what Gabrielle Chanel did to jersey'); 'Tessuto' (Italian for fabric) is the reseller term for the classic Prada nylon; first bag application = the Vela backpack 1984 (imagined in the 1970s). Prada Group: 'nylon is an emblem of the brand's DNA.' Triple-sourced: Prada Group + Vogue (Vela 1984) + Fashionphile (Tessuto Nylon, 1984).

name: Re-Nylon (Econyl)
maps_to: Re-Nylon Backpack, Re-Edition 2000/2005, Moon, Symbole lining
note: The recycled/regenerated version of Prada's nylon: ECONYL, a regenerated-nylon yarn made by Aquafil from reclaimed ocean plastic, fishing nets, carpet + industrial waste, recyclable indefinitely; launched 2019 as a capsule of iconic silhouettes, expanded to RTW/footwear 2020. Triple-sourced: Prada Group (official, 2019, ECONYL/Aquafil) + Vogue (Econyl since 2019) + Fashionphile (Re-Nylon range 2019).

name: Inverted-triangle enamel logo plaque
note: THE Prada signature mark: the upside-down triangle stamped 'dal 1913' that fronts the bags; the triangle/triptych motif Mario Prada first used on the house's vintage trunks more than a century ago; Miuccia embedded it in popular culture. Triple-sourced: Vogue (flipped upside down, stamped 'dal 1913', first used by Mario Prada) + Luxe Digital (the upside-down triangle logo) + Fashionphile (Symbole applauds the triangle from Mario Prada trunks).

name: Vitello leathers (Vitello Daino etc.)
note: Prada's calf-leather family ('Vitello' = Italian for calf). Vitello Daino = a soft pebbled deerskin-look calf (on the Dynamique Hobo); other Vitello finishes recur across the line. Single-sourced for Daino (Fashionphile); the Vitello=calf translation is beat knowledge.

name: Spazzolato calf
note: A glossy brushed calf leather, 'slightly less slick than patent', used on the Cleo. Sourced (Vogue).

name: Antique nappa
note: A crinkly nappa leather wrinkled by hand (labor-intensive), used on the Nappa Antique Tote (SS2023). Sourced (Vogue).

name: Raffia
note: Woven natural raffia, used on the summer Logo Tote and seasonal styles. Single-sourced (Fashionphile).

name: Rosa
note: Italian for 'pink'; a flat product-field shade name (e.g. on the Re-Edition 2000 mini). NOT a season-coded house color name - Prada does not name its seasonal colors.

name: Cammeo
note: Italian for 'cameo': a nude/beige; a flat product-field shade name (e.g. on the Re-Edition 2005 mini). NOT a season-coded house color name.

name: Talco
note: Italian for 'talc': a pale off-white; a flat product-field shade name (e.g. on the Dynamique Hobo, 'Talco and Black'). NOT a season-coded house color name.

name: Cherry red / powdery blue + pink (Re-Edition 1995 palette)
note: The Re-Edition 1995 palette, described in plain editorial descriptors (cherry red, slick black, powdery blue + pink), NOT house color names. Sourced (Vogue).

name: Inky black / milky white / powdery pistachio (Cleo palette)
note: The Cleo palette, described editorially (inky black, milky white, powdery pistachio), NOT house color names. Sourced (Vogue).

name: NO per-season color lexicon (honest finding)
note: KEY HONEST FINDING: Prada does NOT publish a per-season color-name dictionary (the Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine/Fendi pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Naming weight sits on the MATERIAL (Saffiano vs nylon/Re-Nylon), the MODEL, and the TRIANGLE plaque; colors are plain descriptors, often flat Italian shade names (Rosa/Cammeo/Talco). The Galleria 'pop colors' special edition (Alex Da Corte / Scarlett Johansson, Spring 2023) is an art-collab capsule, not a named-color system. Do NOT log invented Prada season colors.
